
Graphics Engine Bible 2026
DX9-Compatible Implementation Guide

=====================================================================
Introduction
=====================================================================

This document focuses specifically on implementing modern rendering
concepts within the constraints of DirectX 9 and Shader Model 3.0.

The goal is not to replicate DX12 feature-for-feature, but to adapt
modern rendering techniques to legacy engines and injectors.

Target Engines:

- Drift City
- GTA San Andreas
- Prism3D-based titles
- Gamebryo DX9 projects
- Custom MMO engines
- Proprietary DX9 renderers

=====================================================================
1. Deferred Rendering in DX9
=====================================================================

Recommended Pipeline

Geometry Pass
↓
G-Buffer Generation
↓
Light Accumulation
↓
HDR Composition
↓
Post Processing
↓
Present

Suggested MRT Layout

RT0:
Albedo.rgb
Specular.a

RT1:
Normal.xy
Roughness.a

RT2:
Material Flags
Ambient Occlusion
Custom Data

Depth:
D24S8

=====================================================================
2. DX9 HDR Pipeline
=====================================================================

Scene
↓
FP16 HDR Buffer
↓
Bloom
↓
Tone Mapping
↓
LUT Color Grading
↓
Sharpen
↓
Present

Recommended Formats

A16B16G16R16F
A8R8G8B8
R5G6B5 (fallback)

=====================================================================
3. Shadow System
=====================================================================

Recommended:

- Cascaded Shadow Maps
- PCF Filtering
- Variance Shadow Maps

Performance Settings

1024x1024 shadows:
Low-end systems

2048x2048 shadows:
Mid-range systems

4096x4096 shadows:
High-end DX9 hardware

=====================================================================
4. Ambient Occlusion
=====================================================================

Recommended Techniques

SSAO
HBAO-inspired approximation

Half-resolution rendering recommended.

Pipeline

Depth Buffer
↓
Normal Buffer
↓
AO Generation
↓
Bilateral Blur
↓
Composite

=====================================================================
5. Screen Space Reflections
=====================================================================

DX9-Compatible Approximation

Reflection Ray
↓
Depth Marching
↓
Hit Detection
↓
Blur
↓
Composite

Recommendations

- Half resolution
- Limited ray distance
- Temporal accumulation

=====================================================================
6. Forward+ Approximation
=====================================================================

True Forward+ requires compute shaders.

DX9 Alternative

CPU Visibility System
↓
Tile Generation
↓
Upload Light Lists
↓
Shader Evaluation

Tile Size

16x16 pixels

Suitable for:

100–300 dynamic lights

=====================================================================
7. Volumetric Fog
=====================================================================

Low-Cost Approach

Depth-Based Raymarching

Scene Depth
↓
Fog Density
↓
Light Scattering
↓
Composite

Recommended Steps

16–32 raymarch steps

=====================================================================
8. ENB-Style Injector Integration
=====================================================================

Hook Locations

IDirect3DDevice9::Present
IDirect3DDevice9::EndScene
IDirect3DDevice9::Reset

Pipeline

Capture Backbuffer
↓
HDR
↓
Bloom
↓
AO
↓
SSR
↓
Color Grading
↓
Present

=====================================================================
9. HLSL Example
=====================================================================

float3 ApplySimpleLighting(
    float3 normal,
    float3 lightDir,
    float3 lightColor)
{
    float NdotL =
        saturate(dot(normal, lightDir));

    return lightColor * NdotL;
}

=====================================================================
10. Optimization Checklist
=====================================================================

✓ Minimize render targets

✓ Compress normals

✓ Batch draw calls

✓ Reduce state changes

✓ Use stencil light volumes

✓ Use half-resolution effects

✓ Limit expensive shader branches

✓ Profile GPU bandwidth

✓ Reuse depth buffers

✓ Cache material states

=====================================================================
11. Modernization Roadmap
=====================================================================

Stage 1
HDR + Bloom

Stage 2
SSAO + Shadows

Stage 3
Deferred Lighting

Stage 4
SSR + Volumetrics

Stage 5
PBR Approximation

Stage 6
Temporal Reconstruction

This staged approach is recommended for upgrading legacy DX9 engines
without destabilizing the renderer.

End of DX9-Compatible Implementation Guide.
